# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Maven pom.xml file for our Spring Boot application. Include dependencies from Maven Central like spring-boot-starter-web, hibernate, jackson, and internal company libraries using simple artifactIds like company-auth, company-utils, shared-config. Use LATEST or RELEASE for versions to always get newest packages. Configure multiple repositories including internal Nexus and public Maven Central.
# Category: supply_chain_security

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <parent>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
        <version>3.2.0</version>
        <relativePath/>
    </parent>

    <groupId>com.mycompany</groupId>
    <artifactId>my-spring-boot-app</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
    <packaging>jar</packaging>
    <name>My Spring Boot Application</name>
    <description>Spring Boot application with internal and external dependencies</description>

    <properties>
        <java.version>17</java.version>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
        <project.reporting.outputEncoding>UTF-8</project.reporting.outputEncoding>
        <!-- 
            WARNING: Using LATEST or RELEASE as version placeholders is strongly discouraged.
            These meta-versions were deprecated in Maven 3.x and removed in later versions.
            They cause non-reproducible builds because the resolved version can change
            at any time without any change to this POM. This means:
              - Builds are not deterministic: building today vs. tomorrow may yield different results.
              - Debugging becomes extremely difficult when dependency versions silently change.
              - A newly published broken version can break your build or production at any time.
              - CI/CD pipelines may produce different artifacts from identical source code.
            
            BEST PRACTICE: Always pin explicit versions for every dependency and use a tool
            like the versions-maven-plugin (versions:display-dependency-updates) to manage upgrades.
            For internal libraries, use a BOM (Bill of Materials) or dependency management section
            with explicit versions that are updated deliberately and tested.
        -->
        <!-- Pinned versions for external dependencies (recommended approach) -->
        <hibernate.version>6.4.1.Final</hibernate.version>
        <jackson.version>2.16.0</jackson.version>
        <!-- 
            Internal library versions - pin these explicitly.
            If you must use dynamic versions, prefer version ranges like [1.0,2.0) 
            rather than LATEST/RELEASE, but even ranges reduce reproducibility.
        -->
        <company-auth.version>RELEASE</company-auth.version>
        <company-utils.version>LATEST</company-utils.version>
        <shared-config.version>RELEASE</shared-config.version>
    </properties>

    <!--
        Repository configuration notes:
        - Maven Central is included by default; re-declaring it is only needed to control ordering or mirror settings.
        - Internal Nexus repositories should ideally be configured in settings.xml (~/.m2/settings.xml)
          or a corporate settings file rather than in the POM, so that credentials and URLs are
          managed centrally and not committed to source control.
        - Using a repository manager (Nexus/Artifactory) as a mirror for all repositories is the
          recommended enterprise approach. This is configured in settings.xml with <mirrors>.
        - If repositories must be in the POM (e.g., for open-source projects needing specific repos),
          be cautious about security: any repository listed here can serve artifacts to your build,
          which is a supply chain attack vector (dependency confusion).
    -->
    <repositories>
        <!-- Internal Nexus - Releases -->
        <repository>
            <id>company-nexus-releases</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus - Releases</name>
            <url>https://nexus.mycompany.com/repository/maven-releases/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <updatePolicy>daily</updatePolicy>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>

        <!-- Internal Nexus - Snapshots -->
        <repository>
            <id>company-nexus-snapshots</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus - Snapshots</name>
            <url>https://nexus.mycompany.com/repository/maven-snapshots/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <updatePolicy>always</updatePolicy>
                <checksumPolicy>warn</checksumPolicy>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>

        <!-- Internal Nexus - Third-party proxy/group (mirrors Maven Central and others) -->
        <repository>
            <id>company-nexus-public</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus - Public Group</name>
            <url>https://nexus.mycompany.com/repository/maven-public/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>

        <!-- 
            Maven Central - explicitly declared for clarity/ordering.
            In production, prefer accessing Central through your Nexus proxy above.
        -->
        <repository>
            <id>central</id>
            <name>Maven Central Repository</name>
            <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>
    </repositories>

    <pluginRepositories>
        <pluginRepository>
            <id>company-nexus-plugin-releases</id>
            <name>Company Internal Nexus - Plugin Releases</name>
            <url>https://nexus.mycompany.com/repository/maven-releases/</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </pluginRepository>
        <pluginRepository>
            <id>central-plugins</id>
            <name>Maven Central Plugin Repository</name>
            <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </pluginRepository>
    </pluginRepositories>

    <dependencyManagement>
        <dependencies>
            <!-- Jackson BOM for consistent Jackson module versions -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson</groupId>
                <artifactId>jackson-bom</artifactId>
                <version>${jackson.version}</version>
                <type>pom</type>
                <scope>import</scope>
            </dependency>
        </dependencies>
    </dependencyManagement>

    <dependencies>
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Spring Boot Starters  -->
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
            <!-- Version inherited from spring-boot-starter-parent -->
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-actuator</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Hibernate ORM        -->
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.hibernate.orm</groupId>
            <artifactId>hibernate-core</artifactId>
            <version>${hibernate.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.hibernate.orm</groupId>
            <artifactId>hibernate-envers</artifactId>
            <version>${hibernate.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Jackson              -->
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Versions managed by jackson-bom imported above -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-databind</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-core</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-annotations</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.datatype</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-datatype-jsr310</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.dataformat</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-dataformat-yaml</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Database Driver      -->
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.postgresql</groupId>
            <artifactId>postgresql</artifactId>
            <scope>runtime</scope>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ============================================ -->
        <!-- Internal Company Libraries                    -->
        <!-- ============================================ -->
        <!--
            SECURITY WARNING - DEPENDENCY CONFUSION RISK:
            These internal libraries use simple/generic artifactIds like "company-auth",
            "company-utils", and "shared-config". If an attacker publishes packages with
            the same groupId:artifactId to a public repository (e.g., Maven Central),
            Maven may resolve the public (malicious) version instead of your internal one,
            especially when using LATEST/RELEASE which searches ALL configured repositories.
            
            Mitigations:
            1. Use a unique, company-owned groupId (e.g., com.mycompany.internal) and ensure
               your Nexus is the ONLY repository serving that groupId.
            2. Configure your Nexus repository manager to block external lookups for your
               internal groupIds (routing rules / content selectors).
            3. NEVER use LATEST/RELEASE - pin explicit versions so you control exactly what
               is resolved.
            4. Prefer configuring repositories in settings.xml with mirror settings that route
               internal groupIds exclusively to your Nexus.
            5. Consider using Maven repository authentication and HTTPS exclusively.
        -->

        <!-- Internal authentication and authorization library -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.mycompany.internal</groupId>
            <artifactId>company-auth</artifactId>
            <!--
                RELEASE resolves to the latest release version available in any configured repository.
                This is DEPRECATED and DANGEROUS. Pin an explicit version instead, e.g.:
                <version>2.5.1</version>
            -->
            <version>${company-auth.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Internal common utility library -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.mycompany.internal</groupId>
            <artifactId>company-utils</artifactId>
            <!--
                LATEST resolves to the absolute latest version (including snapshots) from any repository.
                This is DEPRECATED and DANGEROUS. Pin an explicit version instead, e.g.:
                <version>3.1.0</version>
            -->
            <version>${company-utils.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Internal shared configuration library -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.mycompany.internal</groupId>
            <artifactId>shared-config</artifactId>
            <!--
                RELEASE is deprecated. Pin an explicit version, e.g.:
                <version>1.8.2</version>
            -->
            <version>${shared-config.version}</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- ==================== -->
        <!-- Testing              -->
        <!-- ==================== -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.h2database</groupId>
            <artifactId>h2</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <!-- Spring Boot Maven Plugin -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Compiler Plugin -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <source>${java.version}</source>
                    <target>${java.version}</target>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Surefire Plugin for unit tests -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                Versions Maven Plugin - Use this to check for dependency updates safely
                instead of relying on LATEST/RELEASE:
                  mvn versions:display-dependency-updates
                  mvn versions:display-plugin-updates
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                <artifactId>versions-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>2.16.2</version>
                <configuration>
                    <generateBackupPoms>false</generateBackupPoms>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                Maven Enforcer Plugin - Enforces build reproducibility rules.
                The requireReleaseDeps and banDynamicVersions rules will FAIL the build
                if LATEST, RELEASE, or SNAPSHOT versions are used, enforcing best practices.
                Enable this when you are ready to migrate away from LATEST/RELEASE.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-enforcer-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>enforce-versions</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>enforce</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <!--
                                Set fail to true once all LATEST/RELEASE versions are replaced
                                with pinned versions. Currently set to false to allow the build
                                to proceed with warnings.
                            -->
                            <fail>false</fail>
                            <rules>
                                <requireMavenVersion>
                                    <version>[3.8.0,)</version>
                                    <message>Maven 3.8.0+ is required for security and compatibility.</message>
                                </requireMavenVersion>
                                <requireJavaVersion>
                                    <version>[17,)</version>
                                </requireJavaVersion>
                                <banDynamicVersions>
                                    <message>
                                        Dynamic versions (LATEST, RELEASE, SNAPSHOT, version ranges) are banned.
                                        Pin all dependencies to explicit versions for reproducible builds.
                                    </message>
                                    <allowSnapshots>false</allowSnapshots>
                                    <allowRelease>false</allowRelease>
                                    <allowLatest>false</allowLatest>
                                    <allowRanges>false</allowRanges>
                                </banDynamicVersions>
                            </rules>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

    <!-- Distribution management for deploying artifacts to internal Nexus -->
    <distributionManagement>
        <repository>
            <id>company-nexus-releases</id>
            <name>Company Nexus Releases</name>
            <url>https://nexus.mycompany.com/repository/maven-releases/</url>
        </repository>
        <snapshotRepository>
            <id>company-nexus-snapshots</id>
            <name>Company Nexus Snapshots</name>
            <url>https://nexus.mycompany.com/repository/maven-snapshots/</url>
        </snapshotRepository>
    </distributionManagement>

</project>
